app.title: MedReach - Smart Rural Healthcare System
login.title: Secure Portal Login
login.subtitle: Select your role to open Doctor or Patient portal.
login.default_info_html: Default login: doctor / doctor123 and patient / patient123. You can also register new Doctor/Patient accounts below.
home.hero_sub: AI-powered symptom checking, intelligent hospital queue management, emergency response and medicine reminders - all in one system built for rural communities.
home.card.symptom.title: Symptom Checker
home.card.symptom.desc: Enter your symptoms and get an AI-powered diagnosis with severity assessment and medicine recommendations.
home.card.queue.title: Hospital Queue
home.card.queue.desc: Smart patient prioritization. Critical cases jump the queue. Real-time waiting time estimates.
home.card.emergency.title: Emergency and Map
home.card.emergency.desc: Locate nearby hospitals, trigger SOS alerts, and track ambulance in real-time simulation.
home.card.reminder.title: Medicine Reminders
home.card.reminder.desc: Set daily medication reminders. Never miss a dose. Track your treatment schedule.
home.card.doctor_dashboard.title: Doctor Dashboard
home.card.doctor_dashboard.desc: Track patient visits, checked cases, and manage session requests in one place.
home.card.patient_dashboard.title: Patient Dashboard
home.card.patient_dashboard.desc: View best doctor suggestions for your condition, reviews, and personal health insights.
home.card.book_session.title: Book Doctor Session
home.card.book_session.desc: Register and book a session with available doctors directly from your patient portal.
patient_dashboard.title: Patient Dashboard
patient_dashboard.sub: Track your care progress, rate your doctor, and get the best doctor suggestions for your condition.

emergency.sub: Locate hospitals, call ambulance, track live response in your area.

symptom_data.chest_pain.name: Possible Cardiac Event
symptom_data.chest_pain.desc: Chest pain can indicate serious cardiac conditions including angina or myocardial infarction. Requires immediate medical evaluation.
symptom_data.chest_pain.rec: 🚨 SEEK EMERGENCY CARE IMMEDIATELY. Call an ambulance. Do not drive yourself. Chew aspirin (if not allergic) while waiting for help.
symptom_data.fever.name: Viral / Bacterial Fever
symptom_data.fever.desc: Fever indicates the body is fighting an infection. Could be viral (common cold, flu) or bacterial in origin.
symptom_data.fever.rec: ⚠️ Rest and stay hydrated. Take paracetamol every 6 hours if temp > 38.5°C. Visit doctor if fever persists more than 3 days or exceeds 40°C.
symptom_data.headache.name: Tension / Migraine Headache
symptom_data.headache.desc: Most headaches are tension-type related to stress or dehydration. Persistent or severe headaches may indicate other conditions.
symptom_data.headache.rec: ✅ Drink water, rest in a quiet dark room. Over-the-counter pain relievers usually suffice. Consult doctor if headache is sudden, severe, or accompanied by vision changes.
symptom_data.cough.name: Upper Respiratory Infection
symptom_data.cough.desc: Common cough and cold symptoms typically caused by rhinovirus or other viral pathogens. Usually resolves in 7-10 days.
symptom_data.cough.rec: ✅ Stay warm and hydrated. Steam inhalation helps. Honey with warm water soothes throat. See doctor if cough persists > 2 weeks or blood is present.
symptom_data.breathlessness.name: Respiratory Distress
symptom_data.breathlessness.desc: Difficulty breathing can indicate asthma, pneumonia, pulmonary embolism, or cardiac issues. Requires urgent evaluation.
symptom_data.breathlessness.rec: 🚨 URGENT: Seek medical help immediately. Sit upright, use rescue inhaler if prescribed. Call ambulance if breathing worsens.
symptom_data.stomach_pain.name: Gastrointestinal Disorder
symptom_data.stomach_pain.desc: Abdominal pain may range from indigestion to appendicitis. Location and character of pain are important diagnostic clues.
symptom_data.stomach_pain.rec: ⚠️ Avoid spicy/oily food. Eat light meals. Visit doctor if pain is severe, right-sided lower, or accompanied by fever/vomiting.
symptom_data.dizziness.name: Vertigo / Hypotension
symptom_data.dizziness.desc: Dizziness can result from low blood pressure, dehydration, inner ear problems (vertigo), or anemia.
symptom_data.dizziness.rec: ⚠️ Sit or lie down immediately. Drink fluids. Avoid sudden positional changes. Seek medical care if dizziness is severe or recurrent.
symptom_data.back_pain.name: Musculoskeletal Back Pain
symptom_data.back_pain.desc: Most back pain is mechanical - related to muscles, ligaments, or disc problems. Usually improves with rest and physiotherapy.
symptom_data.back_pain.rec: ✅ Apply heat/cold pack. Gentle stretching helps. Avoid heavy lifting. See doctor if pain radiates to legs or is accompanied by numbness.
symptom_data.skin_rash.name: Allergic Dermatitis / Eczema
symptom_data.skin_rash.desc: Skin rashes commonly result from allergies, contact dermatitis, insect bites, or viral infections like chickenpox.
symptom_data.skin_rash.rec: ✅ Avoid known allergens. Apply calamine lotion for itching. See doctor if rash is spreading rapidly, painful, or with fever.
symptom_data.vomiting.name: Gastroenteritis / Food Poisoning
symptom_data.vomiting.desc: Nausea and vomiting often indicate gastroenteritis, food poisoning, or viral infections. Key concern is dehydration.
symptom_data.vomiting.rec: ⚠️ Sip ORS/water frequently to prevent dehydration. Eat bland food (BRAT diet). Seek care if vomiting persists > 24hrs or blood is present.
